name: Rachel Fry
school: Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science
bio: Rachel Fry is a Ph.D. student in Astronomy and Planetary Science at Northern Arizona University, whose research focuses on planetary geology and geomorphology with an emphasis on Mars. Working under Dr. Kristen Bennett, she serves as a science team member on the Mars Science Laboratory’s (Curiosity Rover) MAHLI instrument, where she studies small-scale surface roughness within Gale Crater. She also works with the U.S. Geological Survey’s Astrogeology Science Center as a member of the Planetary Geologic Mapping group, where she contributes to the Lunar Mapping Program (LMAP), map digitization, and methodology testing for mapping impact craters on planetary bodies. Fry combines her GIS and planetary mapping expertise with remote sensing data to characterize planetary surfaces, supporting broader investigations of their geologic history and evolution.
